extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian attacked on the penultimate climb, soloed 15+ kilometers including a descent and final uncategorized climb to take yellow by 20 seconds.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE attacked penultimate categorized climb, not final climb, exploiting long technical descent where peloton couldn't organize chase efficiently.
WELLENS Tim burned group early for KOM points alone, weakening cooperation in break before decisive climbs—prioritizing jersey over stage.
Quick-Step allowed JUMBO-VISMA to control without contributing, then counterpunched when rivals were committed to tempo—classic parasitic sprint team tactics.
VAN AERT Wout sprinted for second at finish despite being 20+ seconds down, revealing JUMBO prioritized bonus seconds over organizing chase.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: soloed 15km after attacking penultimate climb, then held power through descent and uncategorized finish climb—exceptional diesel capacity for punchy rider
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: second overall and best young rider in first Grand Tour suggests crossover athletes underestimated in stage race GC calculations
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: attacked breakaway companions early for KOM jersey, virtually led on GC mid-stage but abandoned stage ambitions for points competition
rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Mike: negative: JUMBO-VISMA lost yellow despite having numbers because Quick-Step refused to share chase workload on complicated finale
